item.title: Re: AUTHOR POST
item.content:encoded: Just confirming that my YouTube handle is AJ Blackwood and the comments on the Exploring Series video about this SCP under that handle are mine.

item.title: Re: AUTHOR POST
item.content:encoded: added yet another bit of fanart today check it out if you're a fan
Aaaaaaaaaaah wtf thank

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just had the most cursed revelation: Alexei was born in '59. He's a Boomer. Or the Eastern Bloc equivalent, anyway. He'd probably be really awkward on Facebook, like the grandma memes.

item.title: Re: AUTHOR POST
item.content:encoded: There's a bunch of fanart of this on my author page that interested parties may like to check out.

item.title: Just so Cool, and Sad...
item.content:encoded: I like the description and character. Well done. Also, really bad ass in its own way. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd just like to say this is one of the most beautiful skips I've ever read. I'm definitely going to integrate this into my head Canon

item.title: Re: +300
item.content:encoded: Congrats man. You did it.
uhhhhhh… holy shit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was an emotional rollercoaster for me. Leaves me feeling a bit sad at the end.

+1

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: I approve of these edits and can't think of any others that would add to the merit of this piece. However, I'm not opposed to other suggestions.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Got quite a few proposals that can work for this article. The first, Macleod has mentioned upthread that the Engineers mentioned in the article are SCP-1000, so we could inline link there. Secondly, the tales War is Child's Play and Person of Interest File #2273/01 do some fantastic world building in addition to what is done in the base article, and could easily be linked to here. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

SCP-2273: Crow, do you not have engineers here, in this world? They are very much not like us. They fear the sun and have coats like beasts, but are smarter than any man; skinny or armored; crow, dog, or Soviet Patriot; any man you may ever meet. They are what has made the modern war possible, for both sides.

To this:

SCP-2273: Crow, do you not have engineers here, in this world? They are very much not like us. They fear the sun and have coats like beasts, but are smarter than any man; skinny or armored; crow, dog, or Soviet Patriot; any man you may ever meet. They are what has made the modern war possible, for both sides.

Changing this:

[FURTHER STATEMENTS REDACTED AS PER HIPPOCRATES PROTOCOL; ACCESS REQUIRES ETHICS COMMITTEE OR OVERSEER PERMISSION]

To this:

[FURTHER STATEMENTS REDACTED AS PER HIPPOCRATES PROTOCOL; ACCESS REQUIRES ETHICS COMMITTEE OR OVERSEER PERMISSION]

Changing this:

SCP-2273 was recovered during investigation of a seismic event and radiation spikes detected outside Danner, Wisconsin on 13 October, 1989. SCP-2273 was found after tracing radio transmissions coming from its location. SCP-2273 was wounded, delirious, and suffering from malnutrition. It offered no resistance to the recovery team. It was moved to Site-17 shortly after its recovery.

To this:

SCP-2273 was recovered during investigation of a seismic event and radiation spikes detected outside Danner, Wisconsin on 13 October, 1989. SCP-2273 was found after tracing radio transmissions coming from its location. SCP-2273 was wounded, delirious, and suffering from malnutrition. It offered no resistance to the recovery team. It was moved to Site-17 shortly after its recovery.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The funny thing is, I wrote this when enlistment was some weird, nebulous future fantasy that hadn't happened yet; I have yet to actually meet any combat vets who have been through anything resembling what Alexei's been through, and I hope no one I currently know personally ever gets put into that situation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dunno if it's my frequent proximity to combat vets (being in the military myself) or just empathy, but this one brought a few tears out of me. Don't remember how I reacted to it the first time I read it a while back, but it gets a +1.

item.title: Re: +200
item.content:encoded: It's a great article, don't forget to give yourself some of the credit! Congrats!

Holy shit. I am… I'm honored. When I first wrote this article, I half expected it to get deleted and half expected it to stagnate with a mediocre rating. I never expected this character to be so well-received by the community (and never thought I'd write about him as much as I do). Thank you so much, guys.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Necropost, and I haven't seen you around for a while but…

spoilers
– hide block

yes

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Tell me that was 426 at the end there, because that's a better crosslink than 1000.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ok I need to say this but I've love this SCP. It's defiantly one of my favourites. All the elements just combine into one strange, and great story. And that cross link, OH MY GOD! MacLeod you have out done yourself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
the feels man, the feels
the feels for the bugman soldier with PTSD.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This gets a lot of very high praise, and I'm both flattered and surprised every time it happens. I put a lot of effort into it, and it would seem that it's more than paid off. I'm glad you enjoyed this!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
WOW, Mac. Excellent writing! And I think I'll bookmark this for future reference in terms of scientific tone. School major perfect!

Have my +1 decorated with a lot of shiny medals!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Huh. Barely saw this.

It's ok, man, I know that not everything I write can be liked by everyone who reads it, and honestly, this isn't the best this concept could be.

However, it's sitting solidly at over +100, so I'm afraid to mess with it and kill something that makes it work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm sorry, Mac, I know this is your most loved creation so far, but I just have several deja lus to other feelsy, melancholic skips here. The writing is good and the anomaly itself rather imaginative, but it really does not leave me with a major impression, so I'll stick to a no vote.

Glad that other people liked it, though.

item.title: Re: +101
item.content:encoded: SENPAI NOTICED MEEEE!!!
Poor guy

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nope. It's a made-up place. But it's in the southeastern corner of the State, if that means anything.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As far as I can tell, there is no such place as Danner, Wisconsin.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Funny story about that. I'd originally included the sort of weapons soldiers like Alexei would carry, but that version of the draft got scrapped. They were basically just BFG versions of regular weapons, the sort of thing you'd find in the game Lost Planet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is great! Absolutely fantastic except for one detail…. I was thinking of something like this >.< Gonna have to scrap it or rework it now XD Love the idea of bio-armour and weaponry, especially when the two are combined into a self contained suit. Very well written interviews as well. More information on the weaponry would be appreciated, I'd like to see what you choose as projectile and propellant…

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
*scratches chin, thinks on this*

How's a biographical tale sound?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am in favor of more exploration of this alternate universe.

item.title: AUTHOR POST
item.content:encoded: Just noting that I tagged this. Staff, if I tagged it incorrectly, feel free to fix it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I saw this in the ideas and brainstormig forum and wasn't sure it would work. The +42 (+43 now ;D) in a few hours have proven me pleasantly wrong.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's very high praise. Thank you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Best skip I've read on the site in a while. Well done!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm a firefighter. I ain't afraid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: you'll burn for this

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: OK, I won't deny knowledge of that one, but this was written with a very different story in mind, so I don't think it qualifies as plagiarism.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very good storytelling with the interviews.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I helped you critique it :c
But, yeah, this is really good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This only left me wanting more. I have to be honest, I wasn't interested at the beginning, but it very quickly pulled me in. The interveiw logs were written very well. I only ask that you in some way expand upon Alexi more. You mentioned doing tales based off of this, and I would love to read those. You got my +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Isn't there a crustacean armored dude similar to this? Yeah, reminded me of this: http://www.scp-wiki.net/scp-1569

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, that helped a lot!

I reread the entry this morning, and I noticed the "extra-universal" tidbit. I missed that the first time I read it.

I really liked this article the first time I read it, but with the added clarity, it's super good!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How did I forget that that was a thing? Dammit.

Well, the idea actually came to me from about a dozen different places, and that was not one of them. If you feel like looking about in my sandbox's page revisions, you'll see that the focus was originally more on the world Alexei comes from and less about Alexei himself. It was basically me just outlining an alternative WWII and the years following, but that version was less readable than this, so I changed it. I might do a tale or something exploring those ideas again, but for now, Alexei stands alone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: … huh. I thought this was a Reimagining of SCP-326.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah. No vocal cords and all that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I got who the engineers were straight away so I think you've definitely hinted enough (I usually don't catch on to references like this all that quickly). This is good. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Man but it's good to see sci-fi scips. I liked this when I first read it, and I like it now. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The white noise is him crying, isn't it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In all the times I've read it, how could I have missed the reference to the "engineers"? By God, you pulled one over me there!

Upvote for that alone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now that's how you do a crosslink. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I remember this in chat. Never got around to critiquing it, sorry. Anyway, I read it just now and have given you an upvote. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wrote a lot of stuff about why things were the way they were in the parallel universe Alexei comes from, but that draft was boring as Hell.

Moar spoilers
– hide block

Basically, the biggest difference between Alexei's world and our world is the fact that the war between humanity and SCP-1000 wasn't as severe in his world. Instead of getting bombed back into the Stone Age, they were just knocked off their high horse. Their cities and technology were left intact, but they went underground, literally. When WWI came around, SCP-1000 was reminded "Hey, holy shit, there's a whole other sapient species up there!" When they saw that we were divided on such intense ideological and nationalist boundaries, they decided to play both sides in order to get payback. They've been keeping that game up at the time of Alexei's recovery for 70 years, just letting humanity wipe itself out.

Does that help?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
First off, this is very well written.

Initially, I wasn't too interested by the concept, but the interviews really caught my attention. The way the story unfolds is very well done.

I do have some questions though, in regards to your spoiler.

+ spoilery type questions
- BEGONE

If I recall correctly, the intelligent culture of SCP-1000 has long been destroyed, leaving them much simpler than they were before humans wiped their minds. Russia has been a communist country for a relatively short time. This skip references a "red army officer," so I assume he's talking about the communist army. How could that be if SCP-1000 has long since been wiped out, memory wise?

Other than that mild confusion (which is probably just me failing to pick up a detail or five), great article, fantastic writing, and good job!

item.title: AUTHOR POST
item.content:encoded:
So yeah. First humanoid. Whoo.

Blame these people for not stopping me when they had the chance:

Decibelle, TwistedGears, Dillinger, Serne, MrWrong, Waterfire, Kalinin, PullThePower, Djoric, EdAWACS, thedeadlymoose, Dr_Leonard, Roget, SARJ, Anaxagoras, Live2Die

Don't ask me what inspired this, just assume it was a fever dream, because I don't even know.

+Spoilers
-You just had to look, didn't you?

The engineers are SCP-1000.
